title: thymus serpyllum var. parviflorus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. parviflorus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herbaceous perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum var. parviflorus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. This herb can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
